strengths: Nearly 20 years of full-stack experience (well beyond the 6+ required), currently doing TypeScript/Node.js product work as a Staff Engineer, with React, MongoDB, and AWS CDK in production at Optilyz
Hands-on AI agent development at re:cinq — building MCP code generators and frameworks for agentic workflows — directly matching the role's rarest requirement

Deep testing and craftsmanship pedigree: TDD/CI/CD/trunk-based development at RIDE, authored test frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), and taught multi-day testing courses — a strong fit for code-review and quality responsibilities
concerns: No explicit Nest.js experience listed; recent frontend work leans on Ember.js more than React
Kotlin absent and Java experience appears dated (2016–2018); Python not mentioned among languages
Authentication/authorization and security best-practices are not explicitly evidenced on the resume
response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned polyglot full-stack engineer whose current work sits squarely on the role's core stack — TypeScript and Node.js — augmented by production React, MongoDB, Docker/Kubernetes, and AWS experience. He is one of the few candidates with genuine, current AI-agent development experience (MCP generators, agentic-workflow frameworks), the role's most differentiating requirement. His long record of TDD, CI/CD, test-framework authorship, and teaching demonstrates exactly the quality-focused, collaborative engineering culture the position calls for; gaps like Nest.js or Kotlin are framework-level details a developer of this breadth has repeatedly shown he can absorb quickly.
